hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified by the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel radii across lithiation states, those in which the stabilizing element X possesses a higher bulk modulus than silicon will demonstrate a quantitatively predictable linear relationship between the bulk modulus contrast (ΔB = B_X − B_Si) and the slope of channel radius versus state-of-charge, such that alloys with ΔB > 50 GPa will exhibit channel radius slopes below 0.3 Å/100% — a threshold unachievable by nanostructuring or coating strategies alone — thereby establishing sublattice mechanical stiffness contrast as an independent, experimentally verifiable design rule for suppressing volumetric instability in silicon-based anodes.
